vulnerability.reference: Automated verification limited to finite test suites cannot guarantee logical soundness of intricate invariants, enabling overfitting without detection.
vulnerability.generated: Developers' verification workflows rely on finite test suites without formal proof, creating susceptibility to logically unsound code that passes tests.

impact.reference: Integration of logically flawed invariants into verification workflows causes false assurances and potential system failures in safety-critical applications.
impact.generated: Undetected logical flaws in integrated invariants cause software defects, undermining verification and downstream system reliability.

dose_response.reference: Overfitting to test cases enables AI-generated invariants that pass tests but are logically unsound, which when integrated into verification workflows exposes developers to false assurances, leading to system failures in safety-critical applications.
dose_response.generated: RL-based code synthesis overfitting to finite test cases, through developers integrating AI-generated invariants into verification workflows, triggers reliance on finite test suites without formal proof, leading to undetected logical flaws that cause software defects, undermining verification and downstream system reliability.
